content.claim: Hungary operates a centralized primary and secondary school system since 2013, with the Klebelsberg School Maintainer Centre and 58 deconcentrated school district directorates controlling all public school closures and staffing, with school directors appointed by the education minister
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Centralization policy is a documented governance fact, but empirical effects of centralization on closure outcomes versus prior municipal control have not been systematically compared
Local 'voice' effects are inferred rather than measured
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
